passages.title: Cloze Test 17-21
passages.text: The project was abandoned not because of a lack of funding, but because the technical challenges proved insurmountable.

questions.question: The catastrophic wildfires in the region have been attributed not only to the prolonged drought but also to poor forest management practices over several decades.

options.A: The regional drought, combined with decades of inadequate forest management, is considered responsible for the devastating wildfires.
options.B: The wildfires were so severe that they caused both a long-term drought and a total collapse of forest management in the area.
options.C: If the forest management had been better over the last few decades, the drought would not have caused such catastrophic wildfires.
options.D: The primary cause of the wildfires was the drought, although poor forest management practices also played a minor role.
options.E: The wildfires in the region were caused by either the extended drought or the ineffective forest management of the past several decades.

questions.question: Hardly had the new economic policy been implemented when the currency began to lose its value against the dollar.

options.A: The currency started depreciating against the dollar just after the introduction of the new economic policy.
options.B: The new economic policy was specifically designed to prevent the currency from losing value against the dollar.
options.C: As soon as the currency lost its value, the government decided to implement a new economic policy.
options.D: The implementation of the new economic policy was delayed because the currency was losing value against the dollar.
options.E: The dollar gained value against the local currency even before the new economic policy was put into effect.

questions.question: Despite the advancements in medical technology, certain autoimmune diseases remain as elusive as they were fifty years ago.

options.A: Modern medical technology has made it easier to treat autoimmune diseases compared to fifty years ago.
options.B: Fifty years ago, medical technology was not advanced enough to identify most autoimmune diseases.
options.C: Medical technology has improved, yet our understanding of some autoimmune diseases hasn't progressed much in fifty years.
options.D: Autoimmune diseases are becoming more common despite the massive technological advancements in medicine.
options.E: Because of the complexity of the immune system, medical technology will never fully understand these diseases.

questions.question: The researchers found that the results were consistent with their hypothesis, yet they cautioned that further replication is necessary.

options.A: Although the results supported their theory, the researchers warned that the study needs to be repeated by others.
options.B: The researchers were surprised to find that the results matched their hypothesis despite the lack of replication.
options.C: The hypothesis was proven correct, making any further studies or replications unnecessary according to the researchers.
options.D: Because the results were inconsistent, the researchers argued that the entire experiment must be replicated.
options.E: Further studies are needed to prove the hypothesis, as the current results were not entirely consistent with it.

questions.question: Had the diplomat been more cautious in his choice of words, the international crisis might have been averted altogether.

options.A: The diplomat was very careful with his words, yet he could not prevent the international crisis from occurring.
options.B: The international crisis was unavoidable regardless of how carefully the diplomat chose his words.
options.C: Because the diplomat did not choose his words carefully enough, an international crisis likely occurred.
options.D: The diplomat's cautious words were the main reason why the international crisis was eventually resolved.
options.E: If there is an international crisis, it is usually because diplomats are not cautious about their language.

questions.question: It is not so much the lack of resources as it is the unequal distribution of them that fuels global poverty.

options.A: Global poverty is caused by both a lack of resources and their unfair distribution across the world.
options.B: The primary cause of global poverty is the shortage of resources rather than how they are shared.
options.C: Global poverty is driven more by how resources are unfairly shared than by a genuine shortage of them.
options.D: If resources were distributed equally, there would still be global poverty due to the lack of resources.
options.E: Resources are so scarce that even an equal distribution would not be enough to end global poverty.

questions.question: The critics were unanimous in their praise for the director's latest film, which they described as a masterpiece of cinematography.

options.A: Most critics agreed that the director's latest film was good, especially the cinematography.
options.B: Every critic praised the director's new movie, calling its cinematography a work of genius.
options.C: The director's latest film received mixed reviews, but the cinematography was generally liked.
options.D: Although the cinematography was a masterpiece, critics did not like the director's latest film.
options.E: Critics rarely agree, but they all thought the director's latest film had poor cinematography.

questions.question: Urbanization has proceeded at such a rapid pace that infrastructure often fails to keep up with the growing population.

options.A: The growth of cities is so fast that the available infrastructure is frequently insufficient for the number of residents.
options.B: Infrastructure development must be faster than urbanization to prevent population growth issues.
options.C: Urbanization and infrastructure development are moving at the same pace in most modern cities.
options.D: Because infrastructure is failing, people are moving away from urban areas to rural ones.
options.E: The population is growing so slowly that the current infrastructure is more than enough for urban areas.

questions.question: Since the 1990s, the internet has fundamentally altered the way in which individuals consume and share information.

options.A: The internet has made it easier to share information, but the way we consume it has stayed the same since the 1990s.
options.B: People have been using the internet to share information since the 1990s, but it has not changed much.
options.C: The basic methods of information consumption have been the same since before the internet was invented in the 1990s.
options.D: The way people get and distribute information has been completely transformed by the internet since the 1990s.
options.E: In the 1990s, the internet was the only way for individuals to share and consume various types of information.

questions.question: Rarely do we encounter a scientific discovery that challenges our most basic assumptions about the universe.

options.A: Scientific discoveries usually confirm what we already know about the universe.
options.B: It is uncommon to find a scientific breakthrough that questions our fundamental beliefs about the cosmos.
options.C: We often find new scientific evidence that changes everything we believe about the universe.
options.D: Scientists are always trying to challenge our assumptions, but they rarely succeed.
options.E: The universe is so complex that no scientific discovery can ever truly challenge our assumptions.

questions.question: The proliferation of fake news on social media platforms has made it increasingly difficult for the public to discern fact from fiction.

options.A: Social media users prefer fiction over facts, which has led to the rise of fake news platforms.
options.B: It is now harder for people to distinguish between truth and lies because of the spread of false information on social media.
options.C: The public is becoming more skilled at identifying fake news despite its rapid spread on social media.
options.D: Fake news is only a problem on social media because people do not want to read factual news anymore.
options.E: If social media platforms removed all fake news, the public would easily be able to tell what is true.

questions.question: The use of fossil fuels is not only detrimental to the environment but also unsustainable in the long run due to their finite nature.

options.A: Fossil fuels are harmful to nature and will eventually run out, making their continued use impossible over time.
options.B: Although fossil fuels are damaging the environment, we have enough of them to last for many centuries.
options.C: Using fossil fuels is the only way to sustain our energy needs despite the environmental damage they cause.
options.D: The environment can recover from fossil fuel use, but the fact that they are finite is the real problem.
options.E: Because fossil fuels are finite, they are actually better for the environment than renewable energy sources.

questions.question: The CEO's resignation was quite unexpected, given that the company had just reported record-breaking profits.

options.A: The company's record profits were the main reason why the CEO decided it was the right time to resign.
options.B: Nobody expected the CEO to leave because the company had recently announced its highest profits ever.
options.C: The CEO resigned because he was unable to maintain the record-breaking profits the company had achieved.
options.D: After the CEO resigned, the company unexpectedly began to report record-breaking profits.
options.E: Even though the company was losing money, the CEO's resignation still came as a surprise to everyone.

questions.question: The Renaissance was characterized by a renewed interest in the classical art and philosophy of Ancient Greece and Rome.

options.A: During the Renaissance, people lost interest in classical art and began focusing on modern philosophy.
options.B: The Renaissance is defined by a revival of interest in the philosophy and art of the ancient Greek and Roman civilizations.
options.C: Ancient Greek and Roman art was actually created during the Renaissance by famous philosophers.
options.D: The Renaissance was a period when Greek and Roman art was destroyed to make room for new styles.
options.E: Classical philosophy was more important than art during the Renaissance, unlike in Ancient Rome.

questions.question: No sooner had the ceasefire been signed than reports of fresh skirmishes began to emerge from the border region.

options.A: The ceasefire was signed only after the skirmishes at the border had finally come to an end.
options.B: As soon as the peace agreement was signed, new fighting was reported to have started at the border.
options.C: Reports of fighting at the border prevented the two sides from signing the ceasefire agreement.
options.D: The ceasefire lasted for a long time before new skirmishes were reported in the border area.
options.E: Fresh skirmishes at the border were the reason why the ceasefire had to be signed immediately.

questions.question: While some experts argue that AI will create new jobs, others fear it will lead to unprecedented levels of unemployment.

options.A: AI will definitely cause mass unemployment, regardless of what some optimistic experts believe.
options.B: There is a disagreement among experts about whether AI will generate new employment or cause massive job losses.
options.C: Experts agree that AI will change the job market, but they are not sure how many people will be affected.
options.D: The creation of new jobs by AI will fortunately offset the high levels of unemployment it also causes.
options.E: Most experts are fearful of AI because they know that it cannot create as many jobs as it destroys.

questions.question: Boşluk 17 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The project failed because there was not enough money to overcome the various technical challenges.
options.B: Even though they had enough money, the team had to stop the project due to unsolvable technical problems.
options.C: If the technical challenges had been easier, the project would have received the funding it needed.
options.D: The technical challenges were difficult, but the real reason the project was abandoned was the lack of funds.
options.E: The project was so technically advanced that it required more funding than was originally available.

questions.question: Boşluk 18 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Le Corbusier is one of the few architects whose influence on the modern skyline is truly significant.
options.B: Many architects have influenced the modern skyline, but Le Corbusier's work is the most famous.
options.C: Le Corbusier had almost no influence on the way modern cities and their skylines look today.
options.D: Very few architects have influenced the appearance of modern skylines more than Le Corbusier has.
options.E: The modern skyline was influenced by many architects, including Le Corbusier, but in different ways.

questions.question: Boşluk 19 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The disease is caused only by environmental triggers, though genetic factors might also be involved.
options.B: Both genetics and environmental triggers are believed to be necessary for the disease to develop.
options.C: Environmental triggers are more important than genetic factors in the development of this specific disease.
options.D: If a person has the right genetic factors, they will develop the disease regardless of the environment.
options.E: The disease cannot develop without genetic factors, even if there are many environmental triggers.

questions.question: Boşluk 20 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The jury took less than an hour to decide because there was a vast amount of evidence against the defendant.
options.B: Although there was a lot of evidence, the jury still needed nearly an hour to find the defendant guilty.
options.C: The defendant was found innocent because the jury felt that the evidence against him was not overwhelming.
options.D: It took the jury less than an hour to review all the evidence, but they could not reach a final verdict.
options.E: The overwhelming evidence against the defendant actually made it harder for the jury to reach a quick verdict.
